stat_of_week: 0.17 - Shota Imanaga's WHIP of 0.17 across 6.0 innings for the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs is the most dominant single-pitcher control performance of the season, allowing just one baserunner all week
thriller_teams: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs vs. Albuquerque Bombers
thriller_note: The Wonderdogs won on the back of elite pitching — 62 K, a 4.30 ERA, and 7 NSVH — despite being outperformed in nearly every offensive category, a one-category margin that keeps both teams looking over their shoulders in the early standings race.
key_storyline: Hiroshima Radiation stormed to an 8–2 demolition of the Canberra Wombats, their most commanding win of the season, powered by Taj Bradley's 17 strikeouts, Tarik Skubal's 14 K, and a league-leading 6 quality starts that signals their rotation has fully arrived. The Wombats, despite Gunnar Henderson going 4 HR and Jacob deGrom pitching brilliantly, were simply overwhelmed across the pitching categories, dropping them to the bottom tier. After back-to-back disappointing weeks, Hiroshima looks like a legitimate threat — their 8 category wins this week tied Sugar Land for the week's best mark, and the power rankings can no longer keep them in the middle of the pack.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers carry the highest luck delta in the league at +0.91 this week, winning 6–3 over Riverside Roughnecks despite a 5.53 ERA, only 34 strikeouts, and zero quality starts from their pitching staff — the category wins simply did not reflect the depth of their pitching struggles.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs posted a 0.17 WHIP from Imanaga, 62 team strikeouts, 7 NSVH, and a 4.30 ERA — a dominant pitching week by any measure — yet nearly lost to the Albuquerque Bombers and carry the worst luck delta in the league at -0.60, meaning their strong performances continue to produce far fewer wins than they deserve.
records_broken: North Correa tied the season record for most stolen bases in a week with 11 SB, matching the mark they themselves set earlier this season in week 3.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Tied for the most category wins this week with 8, Michael Wacha was nearly unhittable (0.60 ERA, 0.73 WHIP), Max Muncy hit 3 HR, and the offense continues to lead the league in consistency across all three weeks — nobody has been better | same

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Taj Bradley (17 K), Tarik Skubal (14 K), and 6 quality starts powered an 8–2 beatdown; the rotation has arrived, Bryce Harper is posting a .640 OBP, and this team is no longer a dark horse — they are a genuine title contender | up

3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker erupted for 5 HR and 6 RBI, Logan Gilbert was sharp, and the team won 5–3 with balanced contributions across the board; three straight winning weeks confirm this roster is deep and well-managed | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 R, .655 OBP) and Willson Contreras (2 HR, 9 RBI, .607 OBP) were among the best performers in the entire league this week, and the 6–3 win shows the offense can carry games even when pitching is inconsistent | up

5. General Hospital | A 4–4 tie against a Buttville team posting a 2.25 ERA is painful, and the rotation's 5.48 ERA is alarming; 70 K and 5 NSVH keep the floor high, but Cam Schlittler and Michael Soroka need to stabilize for this team to reclaim their ceiling | down

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost a tight 5–4 battle despite Brice Turang posting a .474 OBP and the offense scoring 30 runs — they are clearly more competitive than their record suggests, and four aggressive waiver adds signal a manager who is not sitting still | same

7. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani posted 3 HR and a .467 OBP, Oneil Cruz stole 6 bases, and 11 stolen bases as a team is elite multi-category production; a 5–3 loss despite those numbers is frustrating, but the talent level here remains firmly top half | same

8. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 over the Benchwarmers with Trevor Story quietly posting 9 RBI and Jackson Merrill chipping in 3 SB, but zero quality starts is a structural problem that will catch up to them in a tougher matchup | down

9. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz posted 2 HR, 4 SB, and a .438 OBP, MacKenzie Gore's 0.60 WHIP was electric, and the team ERA of 2.25 was legitimately excellent — a 4–4 tie against General Hospital feels like a win in disguise | up

10. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Imanaga's 0.17 WHIP, 62 team K, and 7 NSVH are all real weapons, and the waiver additions of Chase Dollander and multiple bullpen arms show a manager retooling aggressively; the -0.60 luck delta cannot last forever | up

11. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8–2 to the Skeeters despite solid individual performances from Brandon Nimmo, Yordan Alvarez, and José Ramírez — 75 team strikeouts and a 3.99 ERA show real pitching quality, but the offense has yet to string together a complete week | down

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 to the Dragons but Kris Bubic posted a 0.43 WHIP and Brandon Woodruff bounced back — the pitching staff ERA of 1.65 is the best mark in any losing team this week and shows the rotation has genuine depth when healthy | same

13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 4 HR, deGrom posted a 1.64 ERA, and the team still got blown out 8–2; adding JR Ritchie while dropping Roki Sasaki is a puzzling move for a team that desperately needed rotation upside | down

14. Benchwarmers | The +0.91 luck delta screams unsustainability — 34 K, a 5.53 ERA, and no quality starts are the hallmarks of a team about to fall hard; facing General Hospital next week is the worst possible draw for a roster running on fumes and fortune | same
